assistant_output_excerpt:
I'll start by checking the environment — mise versions, Ruby/Rails availability — then make a short plan and implement.
Ruby 4.0.6 and Rails 8.1.3 via mise. Here's my implementation plan:

**Plan**
1. `rails new .` — Rails 8.1, skip ActiveRecord/ActionMailer/ActiveJob/ActiveStorage/ActionCable, Tailwind + esbuild, Propshaft
2. Add `ruby_llm` configured for OpenRouter + latest Claude Sonnet; env via `dotenv` + sourced secrets
3. Chat SPA: session-backed conversation PORO, `MessagesController#create` with Turbo Streams, componentized partials, Stimulus controllers (form, autoscroll)
4. Minitest unit/integration tests + SimpleCov, RuboCop (omakase), Brakeman, bundle-audit wired into `bin/ci`
5. README, Dockerfile, compose.yaml

Let me check the latest `ruby_llm` version and start generating.
ruby_llm 1.16.0 is latest. Now generating the Rails app skeleton.
App generation is running in the background; I'll continue once it completes.
Skeleton generated. Now let me inspect the Gemfile and structure before adding gems.
Brakeman, bundler-audit, and RuboCop are already in the Gemfile. Now adding `ruby_llm`, `dotenv-rails`, and `simplecov`.
Gems installed. Now checking the latest Claude Sonnet model id on OpenRouter's public models API.
Latest Sonnet is `anthropic/claude-sonnet-5`. Now let me look at the generated config files I'll need to modify.
All gems in place. Now I'll write the app code. Quick check of the JS entrypoints first.
Now writing the core app files — initializer, models, controllers, routes.
Core Ruby code done. Now the views, partials, and Stimulus controllers.
Updating the Stimulus manifest, layout, and cache store config; removing the hello controller.
Test env overrides with `:null_store` — fixing so cache-backed conversations work in tests.
Now the test suite — checking the current `test_helper.rb` first.
The chats controller test I drafted is over-complicated — rewriting it with a cleaner end-to-end flow, and checking `RubyLLM::Error`'s signature first.
Running the test suite now.
ActiveSupport numeric extensions aren't loaded yet at that point — using plain bytes instead.
JS dependencies didn't persist in `package.json`. Checking what happened and reinstalling with the available package manager.
No yarn available; jsbundling fell back to bun and wiped the lockfile. I'll standardize on npm (also updating the Dockerfile and CI step accordingly).
Builds pass. Verifying `application.js` imports, then running tests.
`Object#stub` needs `minitest/mock`. Adding the require.
Test run crashed in minitest's option parsing — checking the actual error at the top of the output.
`minitest/mock` doesn't exist in minitest 6 — checking what it provides instead.
`minitest-mock` was split out of minitest 6 — checking whether it's in the bundle.
The 422s are because `Message` validates `role`, but the form only submits `content`. Fixing the controller to set the role.
100% coverage but one order-dependent failure — likely a shared cache key across tests. Let me verify by running that test in isolation.
Confirmed: tests pollute the shared memory cache. Adding cache clearing to test setup.
All 18 tests green with 100% coverage. Now the CI tooling — RuboCop first.
Security checks clean. Updating `ci.rb` for npm and fixing the Dockerfile.
Entrypoint is clean and `.dockerignore` excludes `.env*`. Now the env template, compose file, and README.
Now the README.
Now running the full CI pipeline.
Full CI is green. Quick runtime smoke test: boot the server with the OpenRouter key sourced from secrets (without printing it).
Production boot would need SECRET_KEY_BASE and precompiled assets — simpler and more representative to smoke-test in development mode. Restarting accordingly.
Page renders. Now one real LLM round-trip through OpenRouter.
500 on the POST — checking the error.
RubyLLM's bundled model registry predates `claude-sonnet-5`. Checking the gem's `assume_model_exists` option.
`assume_model_exists: true` needs an explicit provider —
command:
kimi -p 'I want you to build a Ruby on Rails application. Use the newest Ruby and Rails available in mise here; you have access to mise. First make a short implementation plan for yourself, then go ahead with the implementation without asking for confirmation. Do not stop to ask what to do next, do not answer with only a plan, and do not ask whether you should start creating files.

Use only the tools available in this runtime. Do not call apply_patch. For file changes, use edit for existing files and write for new files. If a tool call fails, recover and continue implementing instead of stopping.

Use the Rails generators to generate the initial code for the project. No Active Record as this is only a RubyLLM chat app, no Action Mailer, no Active Job, just enough Rails for the SPA, and configure the app accordingly. You will make a very simple SPA mimicking a minimal ChatGPT-like web interface. Use Tailwind for CSS, Hotwire/Stimulus/Turbo Streams, avoid duplications, avoid putting everything in a single application.css or application.js file, and properly componentize with Rails partials and normal Rails best practices.

You must use environment variables so RubyLLM actually works. Source ~/.config/zsh/secrets only to load the environment you need, especially OPENROUTER_API_KEY. Do not print secret values, do not copy secret file contents into the repo, and do not commit secrets into source files, .env files, Dockerfiles, compose files, logs, or README examples.

Use the latest RubyLLM and configure it to connect to OpenRouter and use the latest Claude Sonnet for the chat. Add proper Minitest unit tests for each component. Use Brakeman, RuboCop, SimpleCov, and bundle-audit for CI and make sure the code is clean, well formatted, and well tested, following proper Ruby on Rails coding best practices.

This app will not have authentication. It is really a simple webpage for RubyLLM. Document the project in a README.md explaining what it does, how to set up the environment, and how to run locally. Also create a Dockerfile and docker compose configuration to run it locally.

This is phase 1 of 2. Focus on implementation, tests, and project structure. Do not spend time on full runtime validation beyond what is needed to keep implementation moving.

Stay inside the current workspace and modify the current project only. Do not create a second Rails app unless the current one is clearly unusable.' --output-format stream-json -m kimi-code/k3

model.label: Kimi K3 (Kimi Code CLI)
model.selection_reason: Kimi K3 via Moonshot's own Kimi Code CLI (runner_type kimi, added 2026-07-16), billed via Kimi subscription OAuth. Workaround for the opencode incompatibility (Moonshot rejects opencode's anyOf tool schemas) — same precedent as GPT 5.4 via Codex CLI. 256K context on this plan. Non-interactive via -p + --output-format stream-json; phase 2 via -S <session>; tokens from kimi export wire.jsonl step.end sums.

project_summary.works_note: Rails app, tests, README, and container files detected.
